name: GLM-5
description: Z.ai's MIT-licensed 744B-parameter MoE (40B active) with 77.8% SWE-bench Verified, 92.7% AIME 2026, and open-source leadership on BrowseComp and agentic benchmarks. Trained on 28.5T tokens with DeepSeek Sparse Attention.

evidence.value: 77.8% resolution rate
Top-tier open-weight coding performance, confirmed by independent harness

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Industry-standard coding benchmarks with independent third-party verification

evidence.value: 92.7% on competition mathematics
86.0% on PhD-level science questions

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Graduate and competition-level reasoning benchmarks requiring multi-step problem solving

evidence.value: Open-source leader on BrowseComp, Vending Bench 2, and MCP-Atlas agentic benchmarks

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Independent agentic and general-capability benchmarking across domains

evidence.value: Stable behavior across agentic trajectories; reproducible deployment recipes published

output_consistency.methodology: Community testing with repeated prompts and long agent runs

evidence.value: Typical first-party API response ~2.6s; DeepSeek Sparse Attention keeps long-context latency manageable

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 ~6.0s across diverse workloads

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: 200K token context window
First-party API generally stable; open weights enable self-hosted redundancy

uptime.methodology: Review of platform availability and self-hosting fallback options

performance_reliability.notes: Among the strongest open-weight models released to date: 77.8% SWE-bench Verified, 92.7% AIME 2026, 86.0% GPQA-Diamond, with independent confirmation of open-source leadership on BrowseComp, Vending Bench 2, and MCP-Atlas.

evidence.value: Safety tuning applied; resilient in agentic browsing benchmarks but no dedicated third-party injection audit published

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Review of safety documentation and community testing against OWASP LLM01 patterns

evidence.value: Standard alignment; open weights allow guardrail removal in derivatives

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Testing against adversarial prompt datasets; deployer-dependent for self-hosted use

evidence.value: Standard data handling on first-party API; full control when self-hosted

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and self-hosting data-control options

evidence.value: Safety post-training; refusal behavior comparable to peer open frontier models

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting; OpenAI-compatible endpoints

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Solid for an open model; no published third-party security audit. Self-hosting shifts responsibility to the deployer.

data_residency.value: China (first-party Z.ai API); any jurisdiction when self-hosted or via Western hosts

evidence.value: Zhipu/Z.ai is a China-based provider; first-party API data processed under Chinese jurisdiction
MIT weights hosted by Western inference providers, enabling non-China residency

data_residency.methodology: Review of provider jurisdiction and third-party hosting options

evidence.value: Standard API data terms; self-hosting removes the concern entirely

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

data_retention.value: Per Z.ai policy on first-party API (China jurisdiction); zero when self-hosted

evidence.value: First-party retention governed by Chinese data regulations; self-hosted deployments retain nothing externally

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and deployment-dependent retention

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; no managed PII tooling

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: No published SOC 2 / HIPAA / GDPR attestations for the first-party API; Western hosts may carry their own certifications

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: MIT-licensed self-hosting gives complete data control and zero external retention

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of self-hosting deployment options enabling zero retention

privacy_compliance.notes: First-party Z.ai API operates under Chinese jurisdiction — a material caveat for Western regulated industries. The unencumbered MIT license makes self-hosting or Western-host deployment a clean mitigation.

evidence.value: Reasoning traces and agentic tool-call logs inspectable; strong MCP-Atlas results reflect transparent tool use

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and trajectory inspectability

evidence.value: Open-source leader on grounded web-research benchmark, indicating disciplined sourcing behavior

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on factual QA and grounded research benchmarks

evidence.value: Limited published bias evaluation

bias_fairness.methodology: Review of published bias benchmarks and community evaluations

evidence.value: Expresses uncertainty adequately; no calibrated confidence outputs

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.source: Hugging Face model card and GitHub repo
evidence.value: Detailed disclosure: 744B/40B MoE, 256 experts, DeepSeek Sparse Attention, 28.5T pretraining tokens, full benchmark tables

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: Pretraining scale (28.5T tokens) and recipe outlined; detailed data sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Built-in safety tuning; deployers of open weights must layer their own guardrails

trust_transparency.notes: Above-average transparency for an open frontier model: architecture, training scale, and benchmarks well documented with independent verification. Bias and safety evaluations remain thin.

evidence.value: OpenAI-compatible API with streaming, tool calling, structured output

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official repos with deployment recipes; OpenAI-compatible so mainstream SDKs work

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Fast cadence: GLM-5.1 API launched 2026-03-27 with weights on 2026-04-07, same architecture; GLM-5 weights remain available
GLM-5.2 released mid-June 2026: MIT-licensed 744B/40B MoE with a 1M-token context window, priced at $1.40/$4.40 per 1M; GLM-5 remains served and its weights remain available

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning practices and weight availability across releases

evidence.value: Basic usage dashboard; self-hosted observability is deployer-built

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: Active GitHub support and documentation; limited English-language enterprise support

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Day-one vLLM/SGLang support, OpenRouter and major Western host availability

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party hosting, integrations, and tooling

evidence.value: Unencumbered MIT license, unrestricted commercial use and derivatives

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Clean MIT licensing and strong ecosystem support. Note the rapid successor cadence: GLM-5.1 shipped within two months and GLM-5.2 (1M context, MIT) within four; evaluate which version your hosts actually serve. GLM-5 weights remain available.

code-generation.notes: 77.8% SWE-bench Verified with independent verification; best-in-class open-weight coding at ~$0.60/$1.92 per 1M.

customer-support.notes: Capable and inexpensive, but not specialized for support workflows.

content-creation.notes: Strong long-form generation at very low cost.

data-analysis.notes: Excellent mathematical reasoning (92.7% AIME 2026) and agentic tool use for analysis pipelines.

research-assistant.notes: Open-source leader on BrowseComp and MCP-Atlas; strong grounded web research.

legal-compliance.notes: China-jurisdiction first-party API and absent Western certifications are blockers unless self-hosted.

healthcare.notes: Not recommended via first-party API; self-hosted deployment in a compliant environment is the only viable path.

financial-analysis.notes: Top-tier quantitative reasoning; regulated firms should self-host or use certified Western hosts.

education.notes: Outstanding math and science tutoring (92.7% AIME, 86.0% GPQA-Diamond) at budget pricing.

creative-writing.notes: Competent prose; optimized for reasoning and agentic work rather than creative style.

strengths: Frontier-competitive results: 77.8% SWE-bench Verified, 92.7% AIME 2026, 86.0% GPQA-Diamond
Independently verified open-source leadership on BrowseComp, Vending Bench 2, and MCP-Atlas
Unencumbered MIT license with full self-hosting rights
Efficient inference: 40B active of 744B total with DeepSeek Sparse Attention
Very competitive API pricing (~$0.60/$1.92 per 1M tokens)
Well-documented training scale (28.5T tokens) and architecture
limitations: First-party Z.ai API processes data under Chinese jurisdiction with limited Western compliance certifications
Text-only — no vision or audio modalities
Rapid successor cadence (GLM-5.1 within two months, GLM-5.2 with 1M context by June 2026) creates version-tracking overhead
Limited published bias, safety, and red-team evaluations
Self-hosting a 744B MoE requires substantial GPU infrastructure
English-language enterprise support is thin compared to Western providers
best_for: Cost-sensitive coding and agentic engineering at near-frontier quality
Grounded web research and browsing agents (BrowseComp open-source leader)
Organizations wanting clean MIT-licensed weights for self-hosted data control
not_recommended_for: Regulated Western workloads (healthcare, legal, finance) on the first-party API
Multimodal applications requiring image or audio input
Teams without GPU infrastructure who also cannot accept China-jurisdiction processing

pricing.notes: First-party Z.ai API pricing, re-confirmed July 2026; third-party hosts vary. Successors are pricier: GLM-5.1 ~$0.97/$3.04 and GLM-5.2 $1.40/$4.40 per 1M, leaving GLM-5 as the budget option in the family.

metadata.architecture: Mixture-of-Experts: 744B total / 40B active parameters, 256 experts, DeepSeek Sparse Attention; 28.5T pretraining tokens
